# EXODUS — Polish Plan

**Goal:** take v0.7.5 from "tech demo with menus" to something that looks and feels intentionally designed. Without external art assets — every visual is generated by code. AAA polish isn't possible solo without an art team, but **"intentional indie"** is.

This doc is the contract. Each phase has scope, code locations, LOC budgets, and acceptance criteria. Each phase is its own commit (or set of commits) that improves something the player can see, hear, or feel.

Reference targets for visual ambition: **Mini Motorways**, **Prison Architect**, **Two Point Hospital**, **Overcooked** — all hit "intentional indie" and one of them was made by 2 people.

---

## Honest baseline (what we have / what's missing)

**Have:**
- Working sim (SFM, panic, fire, flow fields)
- 5 levels
- Daily mode, 8 achievements
- Synthesized audio (square-wave alarm, basic SFX, panic swell, fire crackle)
- Settings (volume, colorblind, reduced motion)
- Debug overlay (F1–F9)

**Missing — the "looks like a debug visualizer" gap:**
- All agents are colored circles
- All buttons are flat rectangles in Arial
- Zero animations (other than the alarm flash and tween fades)
- No particles, no lighting
- Floor tiles are solid colors
- No hover feedback, no click feedback, no idle motion
- No camera work — every shot is a static top-down
- Audio synthesis sounds 1980s; no music; no atmospheric drones
- Levels feel identical (different layouts, same vibe)
- No moments of drama (first fire, level clear, near-miss save)

---

## Five-phase rollout

Each phase is shippable on its own. After each, the game looks meaningfully better than before.

| Phase | Theme | LOC | Rough hours |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Foundation: sprites, tweens, juice | ~600 | 8–12 |
| 2 | Atmosphere: lighting, music, ambient | ~500 | 6–10 |
| 3 | Game feel: micro-interactions everywhere | ~400 | 4–6 |
| 4 | Difficulty + content | ~400 | 6–8 |
| 5 | Retention: streaks, share, modes | ~300 | 4–6 |

---

# Phase 1 — Foundation (~600 LOC)

The biggest visible-quality jump comes here. Replace circles with sprites. Make every interaction feel like *something happens*.

## 1.1 Custom font

**Why:** Arial reads "default web page." A free display font reads "designed."

**How:**
1. In `index.html`, add a Google Fonts link before the stylesheet:
   ```html
   <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Bungee:wght@400&family=Inter:wght@400;700;900&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
   ```
2. Update every `fontFamily` in scenes:
   - Headers/Title: `'Bungee, Arial Black'` (the alarm-y display font)
   - Body/HUD: `'Inter, Arial'`

**Acceptance:** game text uses Bungee for titles + Inter for body. No more Arial.

**LOC:** ~30 (string replacements across MenuScene, DesignScene, SimScene, ResultsScene, SettingsModal, Achievements, TutorialController).

## 1.2 SpriteFactory — procedural texture generation

**Why:** sprites with a head, body, and walk cycle read as people. Circles read as dots.

**Approach:** at boot, generate 16×24 sprite textures via `Phaser.Graphics → generateTexture`, then use `Image` objects in scenes with tint + frame for animation.

**New file:** `js/render/SpriteFactory.js`

**Textures to generate (~15):**
- `agent-normal-walk` (4-frame: legs alternate, body bobs ±1px) — base color, runtime tint applied
- `agent-elderly-walk` (slightly hunched, slower body bob)
- `agent-child-walk` (smaller, faster step rate)
- `agent-wheelchair-roll` (4-frame: wheel rotation)
- `agent-drunk-walk` (4-frame: wobble path)
- `marshal-walk` (vest, hat — distinct silhouette)
- `agent-injured` (single static frame, slumped)
- `tile-floor-1`, `tile-floor-2`, `tile-floor-3` (32×32 with subtle noise variation)
- `tile-wall-stone` (32×32 brick pattern)
- `tile-exit` (32×32 with chevron + glow ring, 4-frame pulse)
- `fire-1` through `fire-4` (32×32 hand-drawn flame frames)
- `sign-bg` + `sign-arrow-N/E/S/W`
- `pa-speaker` (with concentric arc emitters)
- `barrier-segment` (3 variants for natural look)

**Generation pseudocode:**
```js
// js/render/SpriteFactory.js
const SpriteFactory = {
  generate(scene) {
    const g = scene.make.graphics({ x: 0, y: 0, add: false });

    // Agent normal — frame 0 (legs together)
    g.clear();
    g.fillStyle(0x3a2a1a, 1).fillEllipse(8, 22, 10, 3);    // shadow
    g.fillStyle(0xfde68a, 1).fillCircle(8, 6, 3);          // head
    g.lineStyle(1, 0x000000, 0.8).strokeCircle(8, 6, 3);
    g.fillStyle(0xffffff, 1).fillRect(5, 9, 6, 8);         // torso (tinted at runtime)
    g.lineStyle(1, 0x000000, 0.8).strokeRect(5, 9, 6, 8);
    g.fillStyle(0x222222, 1).fillRect(5, 17, 2, 4);        // left leg
    g.fillRect(9, 17, 2, 4);                                // right leg
    g.generateTexture('agent-walk-0', 16, 24);

    // Frame 1 — left leg forward
    g.clear();
    // ... same head/torso, leg positions offset
    g.generateTexture('agent-walk-1', 16, 24);

    // ... frames 2, 3
    // ... other agent types
    // ... environment tiles
    // ... fire frames
    g.destroy();
  }
};
```

**Boot integration:** `MenuScene.preload()` calls `SpriteFactory.generate(this)`.

**Render integration:** in `SimScene._drawAgents()`, replace Graphics primitives with sprite pool:
```js
// On scene create:
this.agentSprites = this.agents.map(a => {
  const s = this.add.image(0, 0, 'agent-walk-0');
  s.setTint(this._agentBaseTint(a));
  s.setData('frame', 0);
  return s;
});

// Each tick:
for (let i = 0; i < this.agents.length; i++) {
  const a = this.agents[i];
  const s = this.agentSprites[i];
  if (a.state === 'ESCAPED') { s.setVisible(false); continue; }
  s.setPosition(this.offsetX + a.x * px, this.offsetY + a.y * px);
  // walk-cycle frame from sim time + position phase (cheap, deterministic)
  const speed = Math.hypot(a.vx, a.vy);
  const frame = speed > 0.1 ? Math.floor(this.simTime * speed * 4) % 4 : 0;
  s.setTexture(`agent-walk-${frame}`);
  // panic tint shifts the body white→red as panic rises
  s.setTint(this._agentTintForPanic(a));
  // facing rotation (use velocity direction)
  if (speed > 0.1) s.setRotation(Math.atan2(a.vy, a.vx) - Math.PI / 2);
}
```

**Acceptance:**
- Agents visibly walk (legs alternate)
- Different demographics look different at a glance
- Marshals are distinguishable from agents at distance
- Performance ≥ 50 fps with 100 agents

**LOC:** ~350 (SpriteFactory) + ~100 (SimScene render rewrite).

## 1.3 Tweens on every UI element

**Why:** static UI feels lifeless. A 100ms tween on hover/click adds quality without changing layout.

**Targets:**
- Every button: scale 1.0 → 1.05 on hover (100ms ease-out), back on out
- Every button: scale → 0.95 → 1.0 on click (120ms total)
- Modals: fade + slide-in from -30px above (300ms)
- Toolbar selection: stroke-width 2 → 4 on selected, plus pulse glow
- Score numbers on Results: count up over 800ms
- Stars on Results: drop in sequence with bounce
- Best Score on menu: gentle 2s breathing pulse

**Helper file:** `js/ui/Juice.js` — utility wrappers for common tweens.

```js
const Juice = {
  hoverScale(scene, obj, factor = 1.05) {
    obj.on('pointerover', () => scene.tweens.add({ targets: obj, scaleX: factor, scaleY: factor, duration: 120, ease: 'Quad.easeOut' }));
    obj.on('pointerout',  () => scene.tweens.add({ targets: obj, scaleX: 1, scaleY: 1, duration: 120, ease: 'Quad.easeOut' }));
  },
  clickPunch(scene, obj) {
    obj.on('pointerdown', () => scene.tweens.add({ targets: obj, scaleX: 0.92, scaleY: 0.92, duration: 80, yoyo: true, ease: 'Quad.easeIn' }));
  },
  slideIn(scene, obj, fromY = -30, duration = 300) {
    const targetY = obj.y;
    obj.y = targetY + fromY;
    obj.alpha = 0;
    scene.tweens.add({ targets: obj, y: targetY, alpha: 1, duration, ease: 'Quart.easeOut' });
  },
  countUp(scene, obj, from, to, duration = 800) {
    const start = { v: from };
    scene.tweens.add({ targets: start, v: to, duration, ease: 'Quad.easeOut',
      onUpdate: () => obj.setText(`${Math.round(start.v)}`) });
  },
  pulse(scene, obj, scale = 1.04, period = 2000) {
    scene.tweens.add({ targets: obj, scaleX: scale, scaleY: scale, duration: period / 2, yoyo: true, repeat: -1, ease: 'Sine.easeInOut' });
  },
};
```

**Acceptance:** every interactive element responds visibly to hover and click. No clicks feel "dead."

**LOC:** ~150 (Juice + applications across all scenes).

## 1.4 Camera shake / flash / zoom

**Why:** cinematic emphasis on key events. Phaser has these built in.

**Hooks:**
- ALARM trigger: `this.cameras.main.shake(400, 0.005)` + `flash(180, 255, 80, 80)`
- First fire ignition: `shake(600, 0.008)` + `flash(150, 255, 120, 0)`
- Agent injury: `shake(150, 0.003)` (subtle, only on first injury per level)
- Level clear: `flash(400, 80, 220, 120)` + slow zoom-out
- Level fail: `shake(800, 0.01)` + desaturate filter (set camera tint to 0x666666)
- Pause: gentle 0.95 scale + slight tint

**File:** add to `SimScene.create()` and `update()`.

**Acceptance:** the moments listed above all have a physical-feeling response.

**LOC:** ~50.

## 1.5 Hit-pause utility

**Why:** brief world-freeze on impactful events sells weight.

**File:** `js/ui/Juice.js` extension:
```js
hitPause(scene, ms = 80) {
  scene.physics?.world?.pause?.();
  // For our custom sim, set a userPaused flag
  scene._frozenUntil = scene.simTime + ms / 1000;
}
```

In SimScene update, skip sim ticks if `simTime < frozenUntil`.

**Hooks:**
- 80ms freeze on alarm trigger
- 50ms freeze on first fire ignition
- 100ms freeze on level clear

**Acceptance:** big events feel impactful, not just visual.

**LOC:** ~30.

## 1.6 Particle systems

**Why:** rising smoke wisps make fire feel real. Crowd-pressure dust at jams shows where the problem is.

**Phaser has built-in particles** (`scene.add.particles`). No assets needed — particle is a procedurally-generated 2px dot or 4×4 puff.

**Effects to add:**
1. **Fire smoke**: emit per fire cell, rising particles, gray with alpha decay over 2s, slight wind drift
2. **Jam dust**: when 5+ agents are within 1m of each other, emit small dust puffs at ground level
3. **Footstep dust**: every other walk frame, behind agent's feet, single tiny puff
4. **Marshal halo sparkle**: gentle gold sparkles drifting around marshal positions
5. **PA sound waves**: rings expanding outward at 0.5 Hz from speaker position
6. **Confetti on clear**: green/gold particles raining from the top of the canvas
7. **Fire-spread frontier sparks**: orange flecks where fire just ignited a new cell

**File:** `js/render/Particles.js` — manages all emitters, hooked into SimScene events.

```js
class ParticleManager {
  constructor(scene) {
    this.scene = scene;
    // Pre-bake particle textures
    this._bakeParticleTextures();
    this.smokeEmitter = this._makeSmokeEmitter();
    this.dustEmitter = this._makeDustEmitter();
    // ...
  }

  _bakeParticleTextures() {
    const g = this.scene.make.graphics({ add: false });
    // 4×4 white puff (we'll tint at runtime)
    g.fillStyle(0xffffff, 1).fillCircle(2, 2, 2);
    g.generateTexture('puff-4', 4, 4);
    // 2×2 spark
    g.clear().fillStyle(0xffffff, 1).fillRect(0, 0, 2, 2);
    g.generateTexture('spark-2', 2, 2);
    g.destroy();
  }

  emitSmokeAt(x, y, intensity) {
    this.smokeEmitter.emitParticleAt(x, y - 6, Math.ceil(intensity * 3));
  }

  // ... other emitters
}
```

**Acceptance:** fire visibly emits smoke. Jams visibly produce dust. Footsteps leave a trail. Level clear has confetti.

**LOC:** ~150.

---

# Phase 2 — Atmosphere (~500 LOC)

Lighting + music transform "diagram" into "place."

## 2.1 Lighting overlay

**Why:** flat-lit scenes feel like spreadsheets. Lighting suggests time of day, mood, danger.

**Approach:** render a fullscreen darkening overlay each frame, then "punch holes" of brightness at light sources via `Phaser.BlendModes.ERASE` or `MULTIPLY`.

Algorithm per frame:
1. Fill the overlay layer with a dark color (e.g., `rgba(0, 0, 30, 0.55)` for nightclub).
2. For each "light source" (exits = warm green, fires = orange, marshal halos = soft white, PA halos = soft yellow), erase a radial gradient from the overlay.
3. Blend the overlay over the scene.

**File:** `js/render/Lighting.js`.

**Per-level ambient color:**
- Café: bright daylight — overlay alpha 0.10, very faint
- Nightclub: deep dark — overlay alpha 0.65, blue-magenta tint
- Subway: cool fluorescent — alpha 0.30, blue-green
- Stadium: stadium-light glare — alpha 0.20 + harsh white spots from above
- Tutorial: golden hour — alpha 0.20 warm

**Add to level config:** `level.ambient = { tint: 0x000020, alpha: 0.55 }`.

**Acceptance:**
- Each level looks visually distinct beyond floor color
- Fire genuinely "lights" surrounding cells
- Exit cells glow visibly even from across the room

**LOC:** ~150.

## 2.2 FM-synth alarm + ambient drone

**Why:** square-wave alarm sounds like a child's toy. FM = real industrial alarm.

**FM synthesis quick recipe:**
```js
const carrier = ctx.createOscillator();
const modulator = ctx.createOscillator();
const modGain = ctx.createGain();
carrier.frequency.value = 800;
modulator.frequency.value = 12;     // modulation rate
modGain.gain.value = 200;            // modulation depth in Hz
modulator.connect(modGain).connect(carrier.frequency);
// Adds harmonic richness — alarm now buzzes/wails
```

**Replace `AudioManager.startAlarm`** with FM-modulated carrier. Add a slow tremolo on top via a second oscillator on the gain node.

**Add ambient drone per level:**
- A low pad tone (50–80 Hz sawtooth → lowpass at 200 Hz) that plays during pre-alarm and softens after alarm
- Different drone freq per level: cafe = warm 60 Hz, nightclub = clubby 70 Hz with subtle 4Hz LFO sidechain

**Acceptance:** alarm sounds actually alarming, not chiptune. Each level has a distinct ambient bed before the alarm fires.

**LOC:** ~150.

## 2.3 Procedural music

**Why:** no music = empty. Music doesn't need to be a recording — generative ambient with WebAudio is plenty.

**Approach:** layered generative music with three intensity tiers (calm / tense / climax), crossfaded by avg crowd panic.

- **Bass layer**: slow root-note arpeggio (1 note every 2s), sine wave through lowpass
- **Pad layer**: slow chord (4-second crossfade between minor → diminished → minor as panic rises)
- **Pulse layer**: 16th-note pulse (only audible at high panic), hi-hat-like filtered noise

All driven by a single 60bpm clock. Tempo doesn't change; only layer mix does.

**File:** `js/audio/MusicManager.js`.

**Acceptance:** music is present from menu onward, smoothly intensifies during sim, calms on results.

**LOC:** ~200.

## 2.4 Animated exit + sign glow

**Why:** the exit is the goal. Make it impossible to miss.

**Tweens on exit cells:**
- Pulse alpha 0.85 → 1.0 → 0.85 every 1.2s
- Chevron arrows on the exit sprite slide outward (toward escape direction) every 0.8s
- Particle "exhale" of green motes drifting outward from the exit

**Sign glow:**
- The sign body pulses at 0.6s interval
- A faint 12m-radius circle pulses outward from each sign at 2s interval, fading

**Acceptance:** exits and signs visually beckon.

**LOC:** ~100.

---

# Phase 3 — Game feel (~400 LOC)

Micro-interactions everywhere. The cumulative effect of 50 small touches.

## 3.1 Score count-up + stars drop

**Why:** numbers that animate feel earned.

In ResultsScene:
- Each breakdown row fades in over 200ms, sequentially (200ms, 400ms, 600ms, 800ms)
- Numbers count up via tween (use `Juice.countUp`)
- Stars drop with `Phaser.Tween` ease-bounce, with a `playSuccess()` chime per star
- Final score arrives last with a thump (camera shake 100ms 0.003)

**Acceptance:** results screen feels like a payoff, not a table.

**LOC:** ~80.

## 3.2 Modal slide transitions

**Why:** instant pop = jarring. 250ms slide = polished.

Replace every modal show/hide with `Juice.slideIn` and a counterpart `slideOut`. Apply to: SettingsModal, AchievementsModal, PauseMenu, TutorialController banner, ResultsScene panel.

**Acceptance:** all modals enter and exit smoothly.

**LOC:** ~50.

## 3.3 Hover tooltips on tools

**Why:** players need to know what each tool does without reading docs.

In DesignScene toolbar:
- Hover a tool → show a tooltip below: tool name + 1-line description + budget remaining
- Tooltip is a small dark panel with a triangle pointer, fades in over 100ms

**Tooltip content:**
- **Marshal**: "Boosts awareness. Calms panic above 70%. Place in chokepoints."
- **Barrier**: "Drag to draw a wall segment. Block hazards or channel flow."
- **Sign**: "Points the way. Press R to rotate. Useful where flow forks."
- **PA**: "Wider radius. Calms panic above 60%. Good for big rooms."

**Acceptance:** new players can figure out tool effects without leaving the design scene.

**LOC:** ~100.

## 3.4 Custom cursors per tool

**Why:** the cursor should reflect what you're about to do.

Use Phaser's `input.setDefaultCursor(url)` with data URL SVGs:
- Default: arrow
- Marshal: arrow + tiny marshal icon attached
- Barrier: small wall icon
- Sign: arrow icon
- PA: speaker icon

Generated procedurally as 32×32 PNGs at boot, encoded as data URLs.

**Acceptance:** cursor visibly changes by tool.

**LOC:** ~80.

## 3.5 Achievement toast polish

**Why:** current toasts are functional but flat.

Improvements:
- Slide in from top-right (not fade in center)
- Background is a horizontal gold gradient (multiple rectangles or a procedural gradient texture)
- Trophy icon animates: rotates 360° over 600ms on appear
- Add a 3-note fanfare via AudioManager (`success` arpeggio, but minor variant)
- Stack vertically when multiple unlock simultaneously (already added; this just polishes)

**Acceptance:** unlocking an achievement is visibly rewarding.

**LOC:** ~80.

## 3.6 Loading transitions

**Why:** even instant scene transitions feel better with a 200ms wipe.

Add a `SceneTransition.js` utility:
- Black wipe-in from left (200ms)
- Wipe-out from right (200ms) when the next scene loads

Hook into every `scene.start()` in the codebase.

**Acceptance:** scene changes look intentional, not abrupt.

**LOC:** ~60.

## 3.7 Idle animations

**Why:** even when nothing is happening, a living game has movement.

- Menu title bobs slightly (1px ±, 3s period)
- Daily challenge card has a gentle pulse on the PLAY button
- Level cards on hover lift up 4px with shadow expansion
- Level cards' star displays sparkle every ~10s if maxed
- Achievement icons gently float (Y ±2px, 4s period)

**Acceptance:** menu screen feels alive without being distracting.

**LOC:** ~50.

---

# Phase 4 — Difficulty + content (~400 LOC)

Make levels feel hand-crafted instead of randomly seeded.

## 4.1 Replace random spawn with hand-tuned positions

**Why:** randomized spawns make every play feel different, but in a chaotic-not-craftable way. Hand-set positions let the designer create scenarios.

For each level, replace `spawn.rect` + `count` with a `spawn.positions` array. Group definitions become explicit:

```js
spawn: {
  groups: [
    { centroid: { x: 4, y: 3 }, count: 4, type: 'normal' },     // family near counter
    { centroid: { x: 8, y: 5 }, count: 2, type: 'elderly' },    // couple by window
    { centroid: { x: 11, y: 4 }, count: 6, type: 'normal' },    // big party
    // ...
  ],
}
```

Keep `spawn.rect` as a legacy fallback. New levels use `groups`.

Authoring 5 levels' groups: ~3 hours per level.

**Acceptance:** each level has consistent spawn placement that designers can tune.

**LOC:** ~50 in `levels/levels.js` (new spawnAgentsForLevel branch) + ~150 across 5 level files.

## 4.2 Tool unlocks per level

**Why:** giving the player every tool from level 1 is overwhelming and trivializes early levels.

Per-level `unlockedTools: ['marshal']`, then progressively add. New `disabledTools` UI state in DesignScene grays out unavailable tools.

Suggested progression:
- Level 00 Tutorial: marshal
- Level 01 Café: + sign
- Level 02 Nightclub: + PA
- Level 03 Subway: + barrier (introduces routing decisions)
- Level 04 Stadium: all four tools

**Acceptance:** new players see only the tools they need; later levels feel like genuine progression.

**LOC:** ~50 in DesignScene + level config.

## 4.3 Locked levels

**Why:** without progression, the level select is a flat menu instead of a journey.

Lock state: `levels[i].unlocked = (i === 0) || (Storage.getScore(levels[i-1].id)?.stars >= 1)`.

Locked cards: grayscale, padlock icon, "complete previous level" tooltip on hover.

**Acceptance:** new players have a guided path; experts can revisit anything they've cleared.

**LOC:** ~80 in MenuScene.

## 4.4 Progressive tutorial overlays

**Why:** the current tutorial has captioned steps but doesn't show the player WHERE to click.

Each tutorial step gains a `highlight: { tile: [x, y] }` or `highlight: { ui: 'alarmButton' }` field. The TutorialController draws an animated pulsing ring around the highlighted target, and a tweening arrow that points at it.

**Acceptance:** tutorial visually directs attention. No reading required to understand what to do.

**LOC:** ~120 in TutorialController.

---

# Phase 5 — Retention (~300 LOC)

The reasons people come back tomorrow.

## 5.1 Daily challenge streak + share

**Why:** "play once" features are weaker than "play daily" features.

- Track `dailyStreak` in localStorage (consecutive UTC days played)
- Show streak counter on the daily card: 🔥 3-day streak
- Share button after results: copy a clipboard scorecard like:
  ```
  EXODUS Daily 2026-04-29
  ★★☆ • 87 points
  Riverside Café
  ```
- "Days until next" countdown after play

**Acceptance:** daily mode has a reason to come back the next day.

**LOC:** ~150.

## 5.2 Achievement icons

**Why:** generic 🔒/🏆 emojis are placeholder.

Generate procedural icon textures at boot: each achievement gets a 64×64 unique icon (silhouette + accent color).

Examples:
- First Steps: footprints
- Star Collector: 5 stars in a fan
- Perfectionist: a perfect 100% circle
- Lifesaver: a life ring
- Nobody Left Behind: chain of figures
- Frugal: a coin
- Whole Toolkit: hammer + wrench + sign + speaker overlapping
- Drill Sergeant: whistle + cap

**Acceptance:** each achievement has visual identity.

**LOC:** ~150 in SpriteFactory.

## 5.3 Multiple modes (stretch)

Beyond standard play:
- **Time attack**: same level, half the timer
- **Fixed budget**: same level, half the budget
- **No-injuries-allowed**: any injury fails the level

Selected from a "Modes" tab on the menu, with separate scoreboards per (level, mode).

**LOC:** ~100 — defer to v0.10+.

---

# Acceptance criteria for the whole plan

The polish push is done when:

1. ✅ A first-time player on Hacker News looks at a screenshot and says "this looks like a real game" instead of "this looks like a sim demo"
2. ✅ A returning player after 24 hours sees their streak counter and considers playing
3. ✅ Audio runs at low volume in the background during work without being annoying
4. ✅ Every action — hover, click, place, alarm, ignite, evac, injure, clear — has audio AND visual feedback
5. ✅ Each level has a distinct visual identity (lighting, ambient color, music intensity)
6. ✅ Score and stars on the results screen feel like a payoff, not a table
7. ✅ ≥55 fps with 100 agents on a 4-year-old laptop

---

# Implementation discipline

- **One phase per branch** off main, merged when all acceptance criteria for that phase pass
- **Cache-bust query bumped** on every commit that touches a script file, so dev cache doesn't lie
- **Smoke test after each phase**: every level, every menu, every modal — 5 minutes of clicking
- **NOTES.md updated** at the end of each phase with commit hash, what landed, what was deferred
- **No going backward**: if a feature is cut from a phase, it moves to a later phase, doesn't get dropped silently

---

# What this plan deliberately doesn't promise

- **Photorealistic art** — without a real artist, the visual ceiling is "stylized indie." That's fine. Mini Motorways is also "stylized indie."
- **Voice-over** — text-to-speech via Web Speech API is possible but probably uncanny. Skip.
- **Multiplayer** — out of scope, requires a backend.
- **Mobile-first UX** — touch already works; deeper mobile-native polish is a separate plan.
- **Localization** — English only.

---

# Total budget

~2,200 LOC across ~30–40 hours of focused work, split over 5 phases. Each phase ships independently and improves the player's perception of quality.

After all 5 phases, the game won't ship at EA. But it will look and feel like something a small studio shipped intentionally — which is the actual achievable target.

*Polish-plan v1, 2026-04-29.*
